AUSTRALIA_RESEARCH_TOPIC.title: How to Find a Research Topic
description: Finding the right research topic is a crucial step that demonstrates the student's seriousness and fit for a postgraduate research program.
**Where to Look:**

list: **University Websites:** Check the "Research" or "Graduate Studies" sections of the target university. They often list current projects, research strengths, and potential topics.
**Faculty Profiles:** Review the profiles of professors in the department of interest. Their recent publications and listed research interests are excellent sources of ideas.
**Academic Journals:** Read recent articles in top journals in the student's field to understand current trends and identify gaps in the research that they could explore.
**Conferences:** Look at the proceedings from recent academic conferences to see what topics are being actively discussed.

description: **What Makes a Good Topic?**

list: **Specific & Focused:** Avoid topics that are too broad. A good topic is narrow enough to be covered thoroughly within the timeframe of the degree.
**Original:** It should aim to contribute something new to the field, even if it's a small or incremental addition.
**Feasible:** Ensure the student has access to the necessary resources (like data, equipment, or archives) to complete the research.
**Aligned:** The topic should align with the expertise of at least one potential supervisor at the university.

AUSTRALIA_FIND_SUPERVISOR.title: How to Find and Contact a Supervisor
description: Securing a supervisor is the most critical step for admission into a research program. This person will guide the thesis and advocate for the application.
**Where to Look:**

list: **University Department Website:** Look for a "Faculty" or "Our People" section. Filter by research area to find professors who match the student's interests.
**Read Their Work:** Once potential supervisors are identified, read some of their recent publications to understand their work deeply. This is crucial for personalizing the email.

description: **How to Make Contact (The First Email):**
The email must be professional, respectful, and demonstrate that the student has done their homework.

list: **Personalized Subject Line:** e.g., "Inquiry Regarding PhD Supervision in [Their Specific Research Area]"
**Formal Salutation:** "Dear Professor [Last Name],"

**The Opening:** State clearly that the student is seeking a supervisor for a Master by Research or PhD. Mention a specific paper or project of theirs that was found interesting and explain *why* it connects to the student's own research idea.
**Briefly Introduce The Project:** In one or two paragraphs, summarize the proposed research topic and its main question.
**Attach Key Documents:** Always attach the student's academic **CV** and a short (1-2 page) **Research Proposal**.
**The "Ask":** End by politely asking if they would be open to discussing the possibility of supervision.

description: **What to Avoid:**

list: **Generic, mass emails:** These are ignored. Personalization is key.
**Asking them to give a topic:** The student is expected to have their own well-thought-out idea.

NOMINATE_AGENCY_INFO.title: Next Step: Nominate the Agency as the Representative
description: Once the student receives an **Offer Letter** from the university, they need to nominate the agency as their representative. This is a crucial step that allows us to formally take over and manage the application.
**Commission and Fees for Research Students**

list: The assigned consultant will first contact the university representative to confirm if a commission is available for Master by Research or PhD students.
**If a commission is available:** The student will not need to pay any additional fees.
**If no commission is available:** A **Service Assistance Fee of PHP 30,000** will be required. This fee covers comprehensive support for the rest of the application journey.

description: **What Happens After Nomination and Payment (if applicable)?**
Once the agency has been nominated and the assistance fee settled (if required), we will:

list: Take over all communication with the university.
Help gather and submit any outstanding documents required to meet the conditions of the offer.
Guide the student through every step of the visa application process until a decision is made.

AUSTRALIA_REACH_OUT_UNIVERSITY.title: How to Prepare an "Expression of Interest"
description: Before a formal application, most universities require an "Expression of Interest" (EOI) email to the relevant department or a potential supervisor. This is the chance to make a strong first impression.
**What to Prepare Before Emailing:**

list: **Academic CV:** An updated CV that highlights academic achievements, any research experience (including the thesis), publications, and relevant work history.

**Draft Research Proposal:** A short (1-2 page) document outlining the proposed area of research. It should include a working title, a brief introduction, the main research questions, and a list of key references. It doesnâ€™t have to be perfect, but it must be well-thought-out.
**Academic Transcripts:** Have official transcripts ready to attach.

description: **The Goal of the First Email**

The goal is to show that the student is a serious, capable candidate who has a clear research direction and has done their homework on the university and its faculty. A well-prepared EOI is the key to getting a positive response.

AUSTRALIA_RESEARCH_SCHOOL_APPLICATION_IN.title: The School Application Process (Research)
description: For a Master by Research or PhD, the student applies **directly to the university**, not through an agent portal.
**Student's Responsibility:**
The student is responsible for preparing and submitting their own application directly on the university's admissions website.
**Key Documents for Direct Submission:**

list: **Completed Application Form:** Filled out on the university's online portal.
**Passport:** A clear, colored copy.
**Academic Transcripts & Diplomas.**
**Updated Academic CV.**
**Research Proposal:** A detailed document outlining the proposed research.
**Supervisor's Details:** Include the name and contact information of the supervisor who has agreed to oversee the research.

description: **After Receiving an Offer Letter:**

Once the student receives an Offer Letter from the university, they must then **nominate the agency as their representative**. This allows the agency to take over and assist with the remaining steps, such as tuition fee payment and visa lodgement.
